reviewTitle: I've taken all of my gear off of Fat Llama
reviewDescription:
I've taken all of my gear off of Fat Llama after just 5 hires. The website itself is not the issue, although they do take an absolutely shocking amount out of your hire fees - something like 1/5th, which is daylight robbery and, in some cases, makes it a pointless waste of time to let things out at all.

The real problem is the users... The larger agencies are fine to deal with - usually respectful and they have an understanding of hire etiquette, but the young kids who are just messing about are just far more hassle than 15 quid here and there could ever be worth to me.  I've had kit come back missing parts already, kids messaging me at 1.25am in the morning. Everything is last minutes and they then harass you via messaging when they don't know how to use something... It's total waste of my already very busy time. 

Fat Llama could really do with introducing a two tiered user system whereby the corporates or larger established agencies sit on one tier while the student types and ultra low-budget 'filmmakers' sit on another - then let me as the hirer decide which tiers my gear is open to. I'd deal with the pros, but not touch the small-timers.

The pros would happily go through stricter verification processes as well, whereas one of the low-budgeters registered less than an hour before hiring and lost a part of the kit. He could well have used a burner phone or SIM card for this weakest of verifications. It's pointless and I'm allowing hundreds of pounds worth of gear to go out to these people.

It's a good idea., just very VERY poorly executed for professionals.

reviewTitle: Fat Llama is an organisation with dubious business practices
reviewDescription: Fat Llama is an organisation with highly dubious business practices. I tried to rent some golf clubs which were not available as they had already been rented. reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi JG,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we're sorry you had this negative using the platform this time round.

At Fat Llama renters have the ability to message lenders to ask about their items availability before you rent the item, we also allow this feature to make sure the handover can be organised smoothly.

Every time you make a request on the platform, your card is pre-authorised for the amount of the rental. This means that the funds are reserved in "Pending" status in your bank account while the request is pending. Only once the owner accepts the request are the funds debited from your account. Therefore, you would have certainly received a refund.

We're sorry that you feel the fees we charge are high. As we do not take any deposits from our users, our fees ensure the smooth running of the platform, the support we provide in our customer service, verifications, resolutions and security teams and for us to protect our Owners with our Owner backed guarantee. We hope you still would choose to us the platform to save money on renting items.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Rob,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review.

Every time you make a request on the platform, your card is pre-authorised for the amount of the rental. This means that the funds are reserved in "Pending" status in your bank account while the request is pending. Only once the owner accepts the request are the funds debited from your account. 

If you do not complete verification or the owner does not accept the request, the funds are released on our end, and it's just up to your bank to put them back in your available balance. We do try and be as clear as possible regarding the fees when you request to rent, as this is displayed before you pay. For any further information please see our FAQ's: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/

Fat Llama

reviewTitle: I had enough and decided to pull my listings from there.
reviewDescription:
I had to give this a miss. I have a proper b2b rental company for some AV. I decided to put some of the surplus equipment from my main business onto fatllama for the retail users. 

Most of the rentors were good. They arrived on time and the returned on time. But some of the users just really took the mick. Agreeing on a certain time - then when I chase up 15-20 mins after that time, they reply back saying will be there in a couple of hours. 

Bear in mind, fatllama is usualy casual hours, evenings & weekends. Single unit rentals as opposed to multiples I did through my own website. It just wasn't worth it for me. 

I just cancelled the last rentor who took the mick with the pickup timing, delisted all my items and I'm going to stick to the business to business trade. Where I can invoice client if they are late. 

The other issue is that fatllama don't give any documentation or delivery/collection receipt to sign. Customers just walk up, hey/hello, here's the goods. bye. 

When I supply stuff to customers I verify their ID and get them to sign the delivery note. It's all a bit casual. 

The insurance policy for the UK is 2 claims and you're done. You cannot have insurance cover with their hiscox policy if you have had more than 2 claims in the last 5 years. Which is fine. I had 0 claims. But they have some guy on their marketing material claiming he makes £4000 a month from fatllama and used it to buy a house.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Abdul,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, we're sorry to see you go. 

Fat Llama is a community based platform, therefore all organising and arranging for timings is up to the users to do between themselves. We have rules regarding late returns that renters need to adhere to and you're within your right to report other users if they do not adhere to these rules. We also have a rigorous verification system to stop anyone coming through that shouldn't be on the platform. 

If you'd like to find out more about this, then please contact us at resolutions@fatllama.com

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

It takes up to 1hour, or more. They ask for a selfie video, then your ID, proof of address, proof of employment status and income, social media accounts ... only official documents will be accepted. I felt violated having to send them my bank statements and client invoice, but had no choice as I have already paid. In the event of their system being hacked or a data breach to an insider, all of your highly personal data that they process and collect are in jeopardy. reviewTitle: HORRENDOUS - 50% Fees, 25% Refunds, Slow Payments, Punishing Compensation System
reviewDescription:
DOUBLE DIPPING - Fat Lama charge an appalling inexplicable 50% Total Fee by taking 25% from the lender and 25% from the borrower. That means, if you rent something for £100, they will make £50 profit - that is absolutely OUTRAGEOUS! I don't know of any other service that charges 50% fees.

Fat Lama’s payment system is insultingly slow. In 2021, when online payments are instant, you can wait up to a WEEK to receive your payment from them! There are no payments processed on the weekend, so if your rental ends on Thursday - you're out of luck, and not going to receive your payment until Wednesday of next week!

REFUND process is also a pain, because they make it intentionally difficult and expensive. If you as a lender offer a Refund to the borrower, Fat Lama still charge the borrower 25% Fee! Can you believe that? So the lender makes nothing, the borrower loses money and they make money for NOTHING. This is an absolutely shameless ripoff.

Refunding someone is not quick and simple - it is unnecessarily obtuse. As a lender, you have no power of decision to refund a borrower with a few clicks, if it’s necessary – instead you’ll have to jump through hoops and contact their customer support with an explanation and wait for their gracious approval.

The Insurance and Compensation system is also not on the customer's side. I've had harrowing discussions with other lenders, who had to endure the awful compensation process. If something is lost or stolen, Fat Lama will go out of their way to weasel their way out of payment. And if they do agree to compensate, they will find the cheapest price on the web for your lost item, even if the price is a lot lower than its real market value in your country.

Fat Lama's idea of p2p gear lending is great, but their draconian fees and anti-consumer policies make it a terrible choice for lenders and borrowers.

For now they have a market Monopoly, and that's a terrible thing from consumers' standpoint. But not for long - already, there are decent and consumer friendly alternatives starting to appear here in the UK.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Tyrss,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We have a cancellation policy in place to protect owners from borrowers cancelling, please see this FAQ for more information: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/2482856-cancellations-policy. We take on board your feedback that this is not something the lender can do from their end once there is an accepted booking, so thank you for this, we will pass this back to our product team.

If you ever have any questions about the Resolutions or Claims process, please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we will be happy to ask any questions.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

reviewTitle: Great but needs better UI for renters/sellers
reviewDescription:
Fat Llama has been great for me as a renter of my drum kit. I joined just 7 days ago and in that time have made over £100 from several rentals. 

However, I've had two technological problems so far. 

Somehow, I'm able to do more through the app than the website. For example, I find it easy to change availability through the app but can't find where to on the website. Another example is those orange lightning bolt things that show up when searching for products (don't know what they mean, firstly), only they are only there on the app and not on the website. 

My second issue is that as a seller I have no analytics to review my earnings. I need some sort of page where I can view my total revenue ever, for the past week/month/year/5 years etc. It should be similar to google adsense analytics for youtube, and I should be able to compare different items I have listed. It should take into account the value of the item, predict an annual revenue per item depending on its price and past popularity, and show a ratio for each item of it's price to revenue generated per year. 

As a renter/seller, I shouldn't have to add up the numbers/create separate savings accounts just to help view how much fat llama is making me. This should be such an easy fix with an 'analytics' tab under my profile. Please implement this as it really is the only thing that makes fat llama not quite what i want it to be.

EDIT:
Just read that they've had £13M invested into the company - How has this not been implemented yet?!
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Fergus,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review. 

We appreciate your feedback and have passed this back to the tech team.

We do in fact have a section where you can see analytics showing earnings, requests, rental acceptance rate and response rate, as well as lifetime earnings. You can view the last 30 days as well as lifetime. You just need to go onto your profile and then click on 'See My Stats'.

If you have any further questions or feedback, please email info@fatllama.com and we will be happy to discuss.

Best regards,

Fat Llama

I've rented with Fat Llama about 10 times now. Absolutely love the service. Connects me with great people as well as offering a budget-friendly solution to renting lots of gear at once. Support is top-tier and the approval process before you're allowed to rent is thorough, which fills me and the renters with confidence that their items are in safe hands. Highly recommend.
Easy to use and a great selection of suppliers to rent from. Will def use again.

reviewTitle: I was looking to rent a quality lens…
reviewDescription: I was looking to rent a quality lens for a weekend's sporting event.  FatLlama was such a great discovery!!  Superb range of quality lenses, local & friendly collection and easy to order.  Peter's service 10/10.  Will use again for future events.

reviewTitle: Great bunch of people on this
reviewDescription: Great bunch of people on this

The app/site is brilliant. For me it puts quality equipment that would normally be cost prohibitive in my hands plus local drop off and pick ups. Essentially you can expand and contract your equipment for jobs and for fun without any hassle and next to no damage to your wallet.
Really easy user experience, managed to rent a drill really easily that saved me a lot of money purchasing! I'd definitely use Fat Llama again.

reviewTitle: Approval by llama for rentals takes too…
reviewDescription: Approval by llama for rentals takes too long in my opinion
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Muhammed,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback.

We take security very seriously, and we make sure each borrower goes through our verification process where they will have to upload certain documents to confirm their identity. This is a process which enables us to maintain a safe environment for both borrowers and lenders here on Fat Llama.

We try and get things moved along as quickly as possible but sometimes due to volumes this may take some time. We greatly appreciate your patience and your business with us.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewDescription: Fatllama is a fantastic service, I always use it to gather together filming kit for shoots. Collecting kit from other creatives is a kind-of networking. Treat the kit you borrow as though it was your own and you won't have any trouble.

reviewTitle: They double the fees 4 A worse service
reviewDescription:
Used to kind of work because customer service were responsive and good at actually helping you quickly when issues arose with borrowers.

Now fees are crazy? 25% to both parties and they to justify this by saying they have improved the service... they have not... they have made it much worse. 

Particularly worse is the support for lenders via customer service, the worst part is they have made it impossible to directly reach customer service easily (and no phone option at all). It’s a classic discouragement tactic.  If an issue arises and you do get through to customer service via the live chat (Maybe) then they have no authorisation to do anything for you now.  They will not contact the borrower your having difficulties with anymore.  Instead you have to fill out a form and email them ~ buy the time the problem is dealt with any late return will already be in the the past and the knock on effects to lost business work or rentals will take place with no assistance from Fat Llama except maybe The promise you will be compensated later. 

The result is your forced to negotiate with whomever is refusing to return your kit, just to get it back because you can’t afford to spend two day getting Fat Llama To lift a finger to help 

The app is super buggy now Since they updated. So what I’m am I getting for this nutty 25%?  Much Less than I got before.   So the fee more than doubleS and the service got terrible in return.  I don’t get this business move
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Steven,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this review, your feedback is valuable to us.

Regarding the website and the app, our product team work hard on fixing the bugs that have been flagged to us. These types of fixes come every few and we hope the next release will have a better usability for you and other users. We have a number of things we are working on as well as fixing bugs and making the user experience smooth and seamless. Now while this is no quick fix, and we greatly appreciate your patience in this time whilst we work on further developments.

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

In regards to reporting a resolutions case, our Resolutions Team work hard on resolving cases and aim to respond as soon as possible to help you with your case.

Your feedback has been taken on board and we hope you continue to use the platform.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewDescription: Very  expensive experience. Enjoyed using the grill. Good customer service. Quick response.

reviewTitle: Great place to rent affordable camera equipment

Trustworthy great equipment hiring from non-commercial rental people!
Great platform for production kit whilst supporting other creatives at the same time by renting directly from the community. Brilliant service.
Excellent service. Great support. Every country should have this platform!

reviewTitle: Great idea, clunky and inefficient platform
reviewDescription:
I am a big fan of this service and the idea behind it. However, I firmly believe the platform is clunky and inefficient, with several issues:
- The app itself is buggy and I cannot describe how many times I had the issue of missing the messages or lenders' responses;
- A number of times there were unanaswered messages by lenders - who are active on the platform. Unanswered messages should be part of rating. 
- The additional checks for renting items whose collective value is above a certain threhshold - this is incredibly inefficient. While I respect and agree with the system of additional checks, I experienced being flagged for additional checks for the exact same rental I did in the past, which needed no checks. Additionally, I was asked "what are you going to use the camera for?". Unacceptable. If you make me state my portfolio account and profession when joining your platform, send my passport, payslip etc - then make it a stringetn check in the begining and ensure checks for above £X amount are done in a sensible way later but quickly. 
- Your response rate for issues is simply terrible.

reviewTitle: SCAM SCAM SCAM do not use Fat Llama
reviewDescription: Ita a SCAM. Do NOT use fat Llama. I wanted to rent some scooters but renter cancelled last minute. The money came out of my account and renter said he has cancelled because he said he hasn't received the payment in his bank account. (Policy says seller receives their money within 1 week). That is not my issue as ive paid on my side. On my APP, It says ready to collect. I told Renter that ive paid and showed screenshot of receipt. He confirms that its approved but wants the money cleard in his bank account. Renter is not responding to any of my messages. I've emailed Llama to complain and I'm waiting for a response. I wanted to rent a scooter for me and my son this weekend as we have been looking forward to it for weeks but no scooter and I've lost almost £50. Thanks Llama

You should have already received all the funds back in your account but if thats not the case, then please get in touch with us.

reviewDescription: Fat Llama has changed the game, for any small budget photo/video work I've done fat llama is usually the first place I look for kit as your always going to find a good price. Just an over all great service !

Borrower: A truly terrible experience all round using this website. I spend the best part of 10-12 hours trying to resolve a very simple issue but in the end i was ignored and had to cancel my rental. The issue was verification, i had given them my drivers licence and recorded a video message for them but because i wouldn't set my Facebook account to non-private for them i could not be verified and so could not rent anything. I contacted there woeful customer service via their chat app but the conversation was so slow with them often taking over 20minutes to response i eventually gave up and asked that my request £20 rental was cancelled as it was simply not worth the effort and stress they had made the process. reviewDescription: We needed a tent urgently as the one we bought hadn't been delivered. We were able to rent a tent near our campsite from a really nice bloke. Really great offering and concept.

reviewTitle: Honest lender review after over a year
reviewDescription:
Going to keep it short and will write a proper detailed long review once we fully leave the platform. 

Concept is good, when we joined, the platform was great. Now, the little nuisance type issues are:

- App is buggy, slow and pretty much unusable, somehow overheats even an iphone 11 pro (they will respond by saying that they are working on improvements, but thats a lie, it has been like that for at least a year), 
- If you want to use the platform just stick your pc browser, it only bugs out in search every once in a while and in activity page when you try to move past page 1.
- If you are planning on being a lender, expect your items to depreciate, really quick (deep scratches on cameras, dust in sensors, etc etc) Fatllama wont cover it, its wear and tear (read their T&Cs) . 
- 4 out of 5 borrowers will be late to return the kit, they are only on time to collect. 
- When borrowing, people will be rude, people will pop kit into ubers when they cannot be asked to return themselves and not tell you (uber does not have the appropriate insurance btw), they will lose cables and little accessories (doesn't sound like an issue but when replacements cost £10 and it happens once a week, its just an additional cost that fatllama will do absolutely nothing about).
- If something gets broken, you better have video proof just before the rental, because once again, borrowers will just try to say that it was given to them that way. If you can't prove it, fatllama will not do anything.

Now onto the major issues:
- FEES, they've recently increased their fees to 25% each way to both borrower and lender, so if you list something for £100 per day, you get £75, borrower pays £125 (+ getting their own hire out insurnace) and fatllama make £50. They will respond with a link to a blog explaining why they've increased the fees, but as all lenders will agree, changing a line of code to amend how weekly discounting works, does not warrant a 10% fee increase each way. It would be understandable if they actually added features to the platform or made it easier to contact them (they do the opposite of that one, chatbot does everything possible to get you to email them rather than speak to a human being). 

- As mentioned above, kit gets broken, A LOT

- Stuff gets stolen, also A LOT, I want to bring examples from my own and other lender experiences (we all speak to each other) but would rather stay anonymous until the American big daddy competitor gets here near the end of the year. Not going to call them by their name but its a grid to share of sorts. Not gonna get into it, will probably get the review removed.  

- Insurance for lenders is a 50/50, sometimes you get paid, sometimes you dont. Late return fee payments are just like betting on 0 in a roulette wheel, they will try to charge the borrowers bank account once and then pass it onto their security team, which means you will not see the late return fee money.

Anyway, I got bored of trying to not use examples to stay anonymous, will post a full one once we leave the platform in full.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Alex,

reviewTitle: Providing little at a large cost
reviewDescription:
Decent platform, buggy app, polite yet poor customer service, questionable insurance, ridiculous fees.

I first rented through FatLlama and had a pleasant experience and now rent out camera equipment of my own through it. I have lost many rentals because they require renters to get their own insurance and prove identity etc (fair enough) but it never gets done quick enough. I have had people return equipment late and instead of getting me the money for the extra days they said "well they've returned it so you're okay". Most recently the increase to 25% of both the renter and borrowers fee is insane! I'm the one who spent money on my equipment, has paid insurance on it and is spending the time arranging collection and pickups. Yes, you are a third party providing some security and the website and app (buggy) for us to use but that is not worthy of taking essentially 40% of the money. I have looked for alternatives but they are lucky they don't have competition.
reviewCompanyResponse:

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback. 

We hope you can appreciate the verification process in terms of protecting yourself as a lender. We always strive to get this done as quickly but efficiently as possible. In terms of late fee's, Fat Llama do not guarantee this value but we will pursue them on your behalf, please see this FAQ for more info: https://intercom.help/fat-llama/en/articles/3415479-report-a-late-return-case

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

We'd like to hear your feedback on the app being buggy, as we always want to improve the experience for the user so please feel free to email resolutions@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewDescription:
It's a good company/idea that's just run by the wrong people.

It's buggy, their insurance is questionable, and support are next to useless on most topics. The ethics of the company are also questionable at best.

After lockdown started I had multiple messages from people trying to rent stuff from me and Fatlama were basically encouraging people to still be out and about renting by not closing/shutting down rentals (as confirmed by support chat). Pure greed during a pandemic.

Fee increase from 15% to 25% per side was a massive no no and a lot of us are looking to move our equipment away with our own rental insurance. If you rent some equipment out for £80 then you only see £60 of that, while the renter is then charged £100. So fatlama making a £40 fee of a £80 rental. Basically they are making 50% off your proposed rental fee which is rediculous.

Buggy, greedy, unethical, but a good concept that would be great in different hands. Fatlama are basically their own worst enemy.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Tony,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback for us. 

In terms of our fees changing, we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully explain a bit further as to why we've mage these changes: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

As a herd community, we advised extra precautions with rentals to ensure the safety and wellbeing of all our users during lockdown, including non-contact handovers and taking advantage of our partnership with the courier company, Gophr at the time for a discount in getting items delivered. We also reintroduced contactless delivery with our delivery partners at Orkestro. 

In times of crisis it is important to stay connected, look after one another, and prioritise our wellbeing. From spending time in isolation, to re-establishing our work-life balances, we were all adapting to a slightly unknown way of life. Fat Llama wanted to be available for users to rent items that would make  life easier throughout lockdown, such as computer accessories, eco-transportation and games.

If you have any further feedback we would be happy to hear this, please email info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewTitle: Zero protection for renter. Definitely think twice before using.
reviewDescription:
I have been using Fat Llama for a while now. I originally thought it was a great service that allowed you to rent equipment for a cheaper price than the big rental companies. That is no longer the case.

I recently got stuck with a £600 bill for damage caused to an item that got damaged during transportation. Fat Llama offer zero insurance for the borrowers and should an accident occur then the borrower is liable to cover all costs of repair or replacement. 

As they repeatedly reminded me, there is a section in the terms and conditions that states the borrower is liable for all damage etc. But you're unable to use the service without accepting the terms and conditions and does anybody really read the terms and conditions? I will look closer at T&Cs from now on before going ahead and using a company like this.

Rent items on Fat Llama at your own risk and read the T&Cs before you do. Accidents happen and they will not help you in any way should one occur.

We have been working with an insurance company and suggesting this to borrowers for higher value items, but we will take on your feedback and work on ways to make users more aware of this.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewTitle: FatLlama is good but fees are brutal
reviewDescription:
FatLlama is legitimate and works well. Their support is good and I've had no problems with rentals so far. However, their new fee structure is punitive. They charge 25% service fee to you and 25% to the renter. This is huge and definitely puts people off. They used to charge 15% to each which I thought was still high but not so bad. Them making 40% on every rental is crazy and you feel taken advantage of a bit since this has increased. I've personally started looking for alternative options for renting my stuff due to these new fees. 

Another downside is it would be good to have more flexibility in regards to the rental such as editing dates etc... when customers contact you as currently you have to chat to support to do this which although they are very good just seems inefficient. The website is also quite clunky. The app is better but I don't think it has all the features that the site as such as generating discount codes but this could be hidden somewhere in the app. I would also say that I don't actually get a huge number of rentals through them so don't feel they're pushing these fees towards heavy advertising of their service to get more people on the site. It probably is fine in London but outside (I'm still in a major city) not so much. Overall they're a good site and I've had good experiences but just me aware of the fees which now feel punitive, I didn't realise at first they were charging me and the renter, and don't expect huge volumes of rentals outside London. I've had about 3 in 3 months.

reviewDescription: Great company, genuine people, transparent pricing
Great, effeciant service and user freindly platform

I submitted a request to borrow some camera equipment. At first, there were some pretty standard verification documents to provide - driving license, proof of address and a video of yourself. I was happy to comply and quite happy that they complete these checks. What then followed was what has compelled me to leave feedback on my experience. First of all, I was asked to submit a proof of income. I chose to submit a payslip and the instructions were to not blur anything. Quite concerning as this includes my NI number. Secondly, they ask to verify you through social media accounts which is incredibly intrusive for those of us with private accounts (and in my opinion does not prove anything - what do my holiday snaps over the past 10 years prove?). Nonetheless I complied and then had what can only be described as a telephone interview on my rationale for requesting the products. All seemed fine and I thought that would be it: They have my license, current address, employer details & salary (which is more than enough to replace the equipment multiple times over), national insurance number, photos of me and my family and a recorded telephone conversation. That should be enough, right? Wrong. My application was rejected with zero reasoning provided, and my account was deleted and locked. I have emailed the company but no response. I have deep concerns about a company that will ask you to provide so much information and hold it on their files and then delete your ability to log in and become unresponsive.
reviewCompanyResponse:

We just want to confirm that your data is not shared with anyone else apart from the Fat Llama team and we at all times, adhere to UK GDPR policies.

We are very sorry to hear you’ve had this experience. We’d be very happy to hear your feedback on how this experience could be improved, if you’d like to email resolutions@fatllama.com to get in touch and discuss this with us.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

We allow you to freely message any Lenders on the platform before you agree to rent their item, so you can discuss and make sure the item is fully available.

reviewDescription: This has has certainly made life easier in every aspects. Lending and borrowing is made soo easy and safe. Cant thank Fatllama enough. I’m a happy llama. :-)

So this is how it Works. You rent an item  you have chosen from a photo, but  in actual fact the lender posts an item of ‘ similar’ appearance which is also broken. 
The recipient informs Fat Llama it is the wrong item and broken. But they state that can’t be the case as the lender has a photo. ARE YOU GETTING THE PICTURE? 
Fat Llama obviously believe their unscrupulous lender and demand payment for an item never received based on the fact they have a picture!
 
Fat Llama opens the doors to invite immoral, unethical scammers in and not only condone these SCAMs but supports them. 

If you like a bit of dodgy dealing this is the place to be. If you rent from here be ready for false accusations because apparently all lenders are honest people and they have a photo, so why wouldn’t they be?

Be prepared, that nice shiny camera you think you are renting might turn out to be a battered old iron in the box, but the photo shows the camera you always wanted to play with. So be prepared to pay up!

Please avoid this scam and don’t believe the lender reviews.

Additionally keep an eye on Experian once you have shared you precious personal ID.

reviewDescription: Easy and efficient. Also support team got back to me in minutes when I encountered a problem

What are you taking a whopping 40% for? I feel that they even try to hide how much they take by showing you one price and the borrower an increased price. It's just confusing. They should be honest 1/ with the real prices and fees and stop splitting it up taking so much from one side and so much from the other, and 2/ say the kit is not insured at all because it practically isn't insured for anything. All this 'your kit is insured up to la la la' well no it isn't because all you did was force some poor broke ass kid to pay for my kit that he broke accidentally. If he could afford to buy my kit then he wouldn't be renting it for peanuts off fat llama. I think it's completely unfair and unjust.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Rachael,

In terms of our fees changing,  we'd like to direct you to our blog article which will hopefully answer some of your questions: https://blog.fatllama.com/2020/07/08/change-in-the-way-weekly-discounts-work-and-change-in-fat-llama-fees/

reviewTitle: Incredible place for all kinds of things to rent
reviewDescription: I've been using Fat Llama since 2018. I've always found it to be an incredible place to look for all kinds of stuff to rent..from camera equipment to props, to random stuff like...a vintage Italian moped! I've yet to have a bad experience from anyone I've rented from. It being peer-to-peer just means it always feels like there's trust on both sides be you a lender or renter. Highly recommend.

As someone looking for goods and services to rent this app is amazing. A unique business model that gives you access to some amazing stuff. 

If you're renting your kit out it's less impressive. Some fairly hefty fees and quite frankly abysmal payout times often see a rental submitted, lent and returned before the money hits your account. It's pretty ridiculous that in 2020 a company holds funds for 3-4 days before payment. For something that is supposed to disrupt the norm the banking practices are straight out of 1972.

A nice idea but the execution needs work to make this seamless for both sides of the equation.

*Updated after reply from Fat Llama*

Firstly, absolutely how dare you advertise the details of my transactions on a public forum. You've no right to inform anyone viewing this post as to whether I have made money from rentals or not. You might want to check yourself before you're on the end of a GDPR breach. 

Secondly, if you'd taken the time to comprehend my review you would see that my gripe stems from the fact that the delay is unreasonable, not that it isn't Outlined in a document. 

As I said, this is a great app as a renter. You've access to All sorts of equipment with ease. As someone renting equipment out you are at the whim of the system. I believe that in 2020 payments should be instant like they are for almost absolutely other purchase in the World. 

You've been given three stars because that's what I feel you're worth. I have informed readers of the pros and cons in a respectable manner. 

Please don't come leaping to your own defence with borderline illegal information about my user experience. Either make your workflow better or be happy with 3 star reviews.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Brendan,

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback. We are sorry that you feel this way.

We make it clear in our FAQ's on 'How much does it cost to rent on Fat Llama?' & 'How and when do lenders get paid?' which you can easily find online, that we take a 15% fee from the rental from the lender and the borrower and it takes 3-4 working days for the lender to be paid. We also share this information when you're notified that you've been paid. These are 'working days' so sometimes weekends and bank holidays can effect the payout times. 

Please get in touch with us to share any further feedback at info@fatllama.com.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewTitle: Requesting more personal info than my bank to open an account
reviewDescription: I was not able to use this company in the end. They were asking for a huge amount of personal data and money before even confirming my account. I was asked for passport, biometric data, company website, personal social media accounts, linked in, tax returns, bank details... the list goes on. I do not trust this company. They did not refund the full amount that I paid and I have not been able to get through to them. Check out their instagram to see what people's experiences have been. However I have heard a friend of a friend said they were fine so perhaps I just had an unusual experience.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi Yasmin,

We are sorry that you've had this experience on Fat Llama. 

We go through this process to confirm your identity. This is a process which enables us to maintain a safe environment for both borrowers and lenders here on Fat Llama. The documents that you send us are not shared with anyone else other than the Fat Llama verification team. We just want to clarify that we do not ask for biometric data upon sign up. 

In regards to information like social media, this can be used to help establish someone’s identity and/or work, which helps to improve their Trustworthy Score so that we can verify the transaction. We also ask for a payslip and proof of address to boost this score as well.

It looks like you had a full conversation with one of our team members on our instant chats giving feedback and thanking them after this. Just to confirm you should have received the full amount and refund, if this isn't the case please get in touch with us as soon as possible for us to help you with this.

We'd be very happy for you to come back on the platform, so please don't hesitate to get in touch.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team

reviewDescription: Fat Llama is a great way to get professional grade equipment. It is so easy to get what you need and there is always someone available to help you out.

Fantastic hire experience. Great product and after support from the lender. Much more affordable than commercial hire shops. 
A great way to try gear and maybe invest in new kit or make better informed decisions. The lender also had vast industry knowledge in the field so there was also good after support. Highly recommend.

ZERO STARS

Violated privacy, shameless company, poor customer service.

I've sent more than 5 types of sensitive documentation which not even a bank would've questioned if I exist/lie about my identity with it, but this company did. I've sent my payslip, driving licence, BANK STATEMENT, a short video of me, Linkedin profile, Facebook profile etc and I was told I still don't fit the criteria?!?! I don't believe there is a criteria, to begin with!!

After 19 hours of verification, I asked for answers and even though they replied pretty quick, I received no real help! After I've been told someone will help me with this verification issue, my account was quick DELETED. At this point, there is no justice to ask for from a team of shameless people. It's hard to be a buyer as they have no shame in asking you for as many verification data as they want (not need) but easy to be a seller on the other hand who only need their phone number and email verified... 

I'm repulsed as this was the worse customer service I've experienced in my life. I've never felt my privacy so violated and to keep in mind, they could open bank accounts with what they ask you to send.  Not even when I had my DBS check done, even they did not ask for so much unreasonable data and they actually had every right!

Read the other comments if you don't believe the level of incompetence and shameless character they actually sell. Disgusting...
reviewCompanyResponse:
Hi there, 

Thank you for taking the time to leave this feedback.

We're sorry that you're unhappy about our verification process. 

We go through this process to confirm your identity. This is a process which enables us to maintain a safe environment for both borrowers and lenders here on Fat Llama. The documents that you send us are not shared with anyone else other than the Fat Llama verification team.

In regards to information like social media, this can be used to help establish someone’s identity and/or work, which helps to improve their Trustworthy Score so that we can verify the transaction. We also ask for a payslip and proof of address to boost this score as well.

If we can't verify your transaction and your Trustworthy Score isn't high enough, we'll cancel the rental request for you and refund you the full amount.  

If you'd like to share some further feedback on how our processes can be improved or if you'd like to discuss your account, then please email resolutions@fatllama.com and we'd be happy to talk to you about this.

Best regards,

The Fat Llama Team
